title: Verify exactly-once with a recovery drill
description: Prove the source-to-sink path neither drops nor duplicates by forcing failures and reconciling counts.
body: Use before claiming exactly-once for a pipeline with side effects. Do not trust configuration flags alone — only a fault-injection drill proves the contract. Actions: feed a counted dataset, kill the job mid-stream to force checkpoint recovery, and reconcile sink output against input for both loss and duplication. Validation signals include an output count exactly matching input after recovery and a sink log showing replays were deduplicated or committed transactionally. Risk boundary: a sink that duplicates on replay corrupts downstream totals; if the drill shows duplicates, deny the exactly-once claim and escalate to make the sink transactional or idempotent.
